title: Ericoides bartlingianum
intro: Ericoides bartlingianum, also known as Barta's Ericoides, is a flowering plant species within the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ericoides bartlingianum include decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used to aid in digestion, relieve coughs, and support immune function.
